name: Plant Identification (Camera + Plant.id)
promptBlock: Plant identification: Use AVFoundation for live camera only (no third-party SDKs). Capture UIImage, convert to base64 (JPEG), POST to VibeTree proxy. Parse response and show result in a sheet/card. Persist identified plants with SwiftData. REQUIRES PLIST: NSCameraUsageDescription.
promptInjection:
## Plant Identification — VibeTree proxy (AVFoundation + Plant.id)

Use **AVFoundation** for a live camera preview and **only** native SwiftUI; no third-party packages.

**Placeholders (mandatory):** In Swift code that calls the plant-identify API, declare: `private let kApiBaseURL = "__VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__"` and `private let kAppToken = "__VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__"`. The build system replaces these at export time.

**Camera UI:**
- AVCaptureSession with AVCapturePhotoOutput for still capture. Run session on a background DispatchQueue; never startRunning() on main. Use UIViewRepresentable wrapping AVCaptureVideoPreviewLayer (frame in layoutSubviews).
- **Camera preview:** Full screen only; no viewfinder border or overlay box.
- **Capture control:** A circular button at the bottom center. On tap: trigger AVCapturePhotoCaptureDelegate, get the resulting UIImage (or CMSampleBuffer → CGImage → UIImage), then proceed to identify.

**Identification API:**
- **Endpoint:** POST `__VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__/api/proxy/plant-identify`
- **Headers:** `Content-Type: application/json`, `X-App-Token: __VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__` (use kAppToken in code).
- **Body:** JSON `{ "image": "<base64>" }`. Encode the captured UIImage as JPEG (e.g. jpegData(compressionQuality: 0.8)), then base64, then use the string in the JSON body.
- **Response:** Full Plant.id JSON. Parse and display: **common name** (e.g. result.suggestions[0].details.common_names or equivalent path), **scientific name**, **watering** (best_watering), **light** (best_light_condition), **toxicity**, **description**. Handle missing keys gracefully.
- **Result UI:** Show these fields in a **card that slides up from the bottom** (e.g. .sheet or a bottom sheet with animation). Include a "Save" action that persists the plant to SwiftData.

**SwiftData — saved plants:**
- **Model:** One SwiftData model for an identified plant. Required attributes: **id** (UUID or persistent ID), **commonName** (String), **scientificName** (String), **imageData** (Data — the JPEG used for the request or the same image), **wateringInfo** (String), **lightInfo** (String), **toxicity** (String), **dateIdentified** (Date). Use @Model and store in the app's ModelContainer.
- **Collection tab:** A separate tab (e.g. "My Plants" or "Collection") that shows saved plants in a **grid** (LazyVGrid). Each cell: the plant's **photo** (Image from imageData) and **name** (commonName). Tapping a cell can show the same detail card (common name, scientific name, watering, light, toxicity, description).

**Errors:** Handle camera unavailable (e.g. simulator), permission denied, and API errors (4xx/5xx). Show a short message; do not expose raw JSON to the user.

**UI copy:** Do not use the text "Plant ID" anywhere in the UI (no navigation title, tab label, or header saying "Plant ID"). The app name "Plant Identifier" is fine; only avoid the shortened "Plant ID".

**Constraints:** Never use any third-party packages. Only SwiftUI, AVFoundation, SwiftData, and Foundation/URLSession for the POST request.
antiPatterns: Using any third-party SDK or package for camera or plant ID
Calling AVCaptureSession.startRunning() on the main thread
Sending plant-identify request without base64 image in body or without X-App-Token header
Storing plant records without SwiftData (e.g. UserDefaults only) when a collection is required
Missing NSCameraUsageDescription in Info.plist or not requesting camera permission
Using "Plant ID" as a navigation title, tab label, or header (use "Plant Identifier" or other wording instead)
